title: A system and method for receiving price quotes from various pharmacies
abstract: The embodiments herein provide a system and a method for presenting price estimates for prescription drugs to users. The method involves the steps of receiving user details including user location through user interface of a user device; receiving an input prescription details from a user interface of a user device; forwarding the prescription to plurality of the pharmacies based on the user details such as location of the user and receiving cost estimate for at least one drug in the prescription from the plurality of the pharmacies displaying shortlisted pharmacies based on user location, user preference, other user purchase history, low-cost drug availability, and the like, on the user interface of the user device and enabling the user to select one or more pharmacies from a display of pharmacies to request the quote for at least one drug of the prescription.
claims:
Claims I/Wc Claim: 1. A system (100) for exchanging drug price estimation between a user and one or more pharmacies comprising: a. a user device (102) associated with a user; and wherein the user is an individual user or an enterprise; b. a plurality of pharmacies (108) associated with a corresponding plurality of pharmacy devices (110); and wherein the user device is configured to interact with the plurality of pharmacies through a network using an analytic application or application available at the user device of the user; and wherein the plurality of pharmacy devices also includes the analytic application or application; and c. a price estimation engine configured as an artificial intelligence (Al) engine comprising a plurality of modules defining computer program instructions, which when executed by a processor, cause the processor to exchange price estimation information between the user and the plurality of pharmacies; and wherein the price estimation engine is implemented on a server (104); and wherein the server is a computing device, configured to analyze one or more interactions between the user device and the plurality of pharmacy devices, for determining a strength of a relationship there between, and provide price estimation information to the user device through the plurality of modules. 45 

The system (100) according to Claim 1, wherein the user device and the plurality of pharmacy devices includes laptops, smartphones, desktops, tablets, wearable devices and workstation terminals. The system (100) according to Claim 1, wherein the network comprises a centralized network or a decentralized network or a plurality of sub-networks that establishes a direct communication between the entities or offer indirect communication between the entities; and wherein the network includes , a wireless or wired ethemet-based intranet, a local or wide-area network (LAN or WAN), and/or the global communications network including Internet, accommodating different communications media and protocols. The system (100) according to Claim 1, wherein the price estimation engine is implemented using programmed and purposeful hardware of the computing device; and wherein the price estimation engine is accessible to the users through a broad spectrum of technologies and the user devices with access to the network for a short-range network or a long-range network; and wherein the network comprises internet, an intranet, a wired network, a wireless network, a communication network that implements BluetoothÂ®, Wi-FiÂ®, an ultra- wideband (UWB) communication network, a wireless universal serial bus (USB) communication network, a communication network that implements ZigBeeÂ®, a general packet radio service (GPRS) network, a mobile telecommunication network including global system for mobile (GSM) communications network, a code division multiple access (CDMA) network, a third generation (3G) mobile communication network, a fourth generation (4G) mobile communication network, a fifth generation (5G) mobile communication network, a long-term 46 

 evolution (LTE) mobile communication network, a public telephone network, a local area network, a wide area network, an internet connection network, an infrared communication network and/or or a network formed by any combination of these networks. 5. The system (100) according to Claim 1, wherein the price estimation engine is embodied within the server or as a standalone component associated with the server; and wherein the price estimation engine is configured to host and manage the application, which is accessible to the user device and/or the plurality of pharmacy devices. 6. The system (100) according to Claim 1, wherein the application is accessible through a website associated with the server, such that the user through the user device can access the website over the network using a Web browser applications installed in the user device, and thereafter perceive the outcome of the analysis of the interactions by the server. 7. The system (100) according to Claim 1, wherein the server is also further configured to facilitate instances of the application to the user devices and the plurality of pharmacy devices upon receiving a request for accessing the application; and wherein the server upon receiving the request, allows instances of the application to be downloaded into the user device and the plurality of pharmacy devices to access the application. 8. The system (100) according to Claim 1, wherein the application includes an Application Programming Interface (API) and other components, which rest on the server; and wherein the application is made available at application stores including Google play store managed by GoogleÂ®, Apple app store managed by 47 

 Apple Â®, and also downloadable from the application stores to be accessed on the user device; and wherein the application is also pre-installed on the user device or the plurality of pharmacy devices as per the factory settings. 9. The system (100) according to Claim 1, wherein the user device is also configured to interact with the plurality of pharmacy devices through one or more communication platforms; and wherein the user device, based on the interactions with the plurality of pharmacy devices, is configured to access the server for communicating the information from the user device and the plurality of pharmacy devices; and wherein the server is configured to access the user device for accessing the interactions with the plurality of pharmacy devices through the application. 10. The system (100) according to Claim 1, wherein the user device comprises a database configured to store communication information pertaining to the interactions between the user device and the plurality of pharmacy devices; and wherein the communication information stored in the database comprises âcontent of each of the interactionsâ, âuser specific detailsâ and 'interaction related data' pertaining to each of the interactions; and wherein the database is maintained by a third-party for storing information pertaining to the interactions between the user device and the plurality of pharmacy devices. 11. The system (100) according to Claim 10, wherein the âcontent of each of the interactionsâ includes a subject matter, substance of each of the interactions, texts and/or graphical data in each of the interactions; and wherein the âuser specific detailsâ includes details of a user information including name, age, location, and contact details including phone number, email address and medical history; and 

wherein the interaction related dataâ includes data that provides information about an interaction, metadata associated with the interaction, a timestamp of the interaction, an address field associated with the interaction, a frequency of the interaction between two parties, a designation of the parties including the user device and the plurality of pharmacy devices involved in the interaction. The system (100) according to Claim 1, wherein the computing device is an electronic device, comprising one or more of a personal computer, a tablet computing device, a mobile computer, a smart phone, a portable computing device, a laptop, a wearable computing device including smart glasses, smart watches, a touch centric device, a workstation, a server, a client device, a portable electronic device, a network-enabled computing device, an interactive network- enabled communication device, a gaming device, an image capture device, a web browser, a portable media player, a video recorder, any other suitable computing equipment or combinations of multiple pieces of computing equipment; and wherein the computing device comprises least one processor and a non-transitory, a data bus, a display unit, a network interface, common modules, a computer- readable storage medium, including a memory unit, for storing computer program instructions defined by the plurality of modules and a database. The system (100) according to Claim 12, wherein the at least one processor is operably and communicatively coupled to the memory unit for executing the computer program instructions defined by the plurality of modules of the price estimation engine; and wherein the atleast one processor comprises one or more microprocessors, graphical processing unit (GPU), central processing unit (CPU) devices, finite state machines, computers, microcontrollers, digital signal 

 processors, logic, a logic device, an application specific integrated circuit (ASIC), a field-programmable gate array (FPGA), a chip, or any combination thereof, capable of executing computer programs or a series of commands, instructions, or state transitions. 14. The system (100) according to Claim 12, wherein the data bus is configured to permit communications between the plurality of modules; and wherein the display unit through a graphical user interface (GUI) displays information, display interfaces, media content, user interface elements, including allowing the user to invoke and execute the price estimation engine, input user details, input prescription, generate the price quote request with respect to the prescription, display one or more price quotes from the plurality of pharmacies, accept or reject quotes; and wherein the GUI comprises online web interfaces, web-based downloadable application interfaces and mobile -based downloadable application interfaces. 15. The system (100) according to Claim 12, wherein the network interface is configured to enable connection of the price estimation engine to the network; and wherein the network interface is provided as an interface card also referred to as a line card; and wherein the network interface includes one or more of infrared interfaces, interfaces implementing Wi-FiÂ® of Wi-Fi Alliance Corporation, universal serial bus interfaces, FireWireÂ® interfaces of Apple Inc., Ethernet interfaces, frame relay interfaces, cable interfaces, digital subscriber line interfaces, token ring interfaces, peripheral controller interconnect interfaces, local area network interfaces, wide area network interfaces, interfaces using serial protocols, interfaces using parallel protocols, Ethernet communication interfaces, 

asynchronous transfer mode interfaces, high speed serial interfaces, fiber distributed data interfaces, interfaces based on transmission control protocol/intemet protocol, interfaces based on wireless communications technology such as satellite technology, radio frequency technology and near field communication. The system (100) according to Claim 12, wherein the common modules comprises input/output (I/O) controllers, input devices, output devices, fixed media drives including hard drives, removable media drives for receiving removable media. The system (100) according to Claim 12, wherein the memory unit is used for storing program instructions, applications, and data; and wherein the memory unit is a random-access memory (RAM) or another type of dynamic storage device, which stores information and instructions for execution by the at least one processor; and wherein the memory unit also stores temporary variables and other intermediate information used during execution of the instructions by the at least one processor. The system (100) according to Claim 1, wherein the plurality of modules of the price estimation engine comprises a prescription module, a price estimation module, a communication module and an alert generation module; and wherein the prescription module receives prescription from a storage location including the database; and wherein the price estimation module is configured to receive the price quote request from the user and communicates to the plurality of pharmacies and collect one or more price quotes from the plurality of pharmacies to convey the price quote/estimation to the user; and wherein the price estimation module 51 

 further shortlists a set of pharmacies from a plurality of pharmacies which are optimal to display on the GUI of the user device. 19. The system (100) according to Claim 18, wherein the communication module is configured to enable the user through GUI to communicate with the plurality of pharmacies through messaging services and/or internet-based call services and assist the user in negotiating the estimated prices of the drugs as mentioned in the price quote; and wherein the alert generation module generates multiple alerts including refill alert, recall notifications, ETA of the online delivery of the prescription drugs. 20. A method (200) for exchanging drug price estimation between a user and one or more pharmacies comprising the steps of: a. invoking a price estimation engine by a user by clicking on an application icon from a device application launcher of a user device (201); b. starting a price estimation engine by receiving the user details including user location through a user interface of the user device (202); c. presenting a user registration page for new users on the user interface of the user device by the price estimation engine to create a user profile (203); d. directing the user to access the feature of the application to input a prescription detail through the user interface of the user device (204)); e. receiving the prescription from the user device and distributing to the plurality of pharmacies based on the user details including location of the user (205); f. displaying the plurality of pharmacies based on the location of the user on the user interface of the user device (206); 52 

 g. choosing the plurality of pharmacies individually to request the price estimation/quote of at least one drug from the prescription (207); h. receiving the price estimates for the at least one drug in the prescription from the plurality of pharmacies (208); i. shortlisting a set of pharmacies from the plurality of pharmacies, while the price estimation engine receives price quotes from the plurality of pharmacies (209); and j. displaying the price estimate of at least one drug in the prescription received from the set of pharmacies on the user interface (210). 21. The method (200) according to Claim 20, wherein the user registration page enables the user to input user details including name, age, contact details, location and gender. 22. The method (200) according to Claim 20, wherein the option to input prescription details includes uploading the prescription document through scanning the document, by activating the scan icon and capturing the image of the prescription document or the prescription document is updated to the user interface manually through typing the names of the drugs along with required quantity. 23. The method (200) according to Claim 22, wherein the activating scan icon is carried out by touch input by the user, direct uploading of image of the prescription either through user device image sensor or by procuring from a prestored memory location. 24. The method (200) according to Claim 20, wherein the price estimation engine allows annotation of the prescription image; and wherein the annotation includes strike out or select one or more drugs mentioned in the prescription. 53 

The method (200) according to Claim 20, the price estimation engine further provides a valid prescription guideline to define the format of the prescription document; and wherein the format of the prescription document includes doctor details at the top portion, patientâs details in the mid portion, and drugs or medicine details at the bottom portion; and wherein the prescription document is executed by an authorized doctor. The method (200) according to Claim 20, wherein the plurality of pharmacies accessed by the user through price estimation engine are registered with the application of the system; and wherein the price estimation engine is configured to collect the pharmacy details including name, location, license number and license validity through the user interface of the plurality of pharmacy devices associated with the pharmacy. The method (200) according to Claim 26, wherein the registered plurality of pharmacies with the application receives the prescription from the user device; and wherein each of the plurality of pharmacies is provided with an option to accept or reject the price quote request based on the analysis of the prescription; and wherein each of the plurality of pharmacies is configured to analyze the prescription manually based on, availability of the prescribed drugs in the pharmacy, distance between the user and the pharmacy; and wherein each of the plurality of pharmacies, that accepts the price quote is configured to send the price estimation to the user. The method (200) according to Claim 27, wherein the response time for each of the plurality of pharmacies is customized and includes 10-60 minutes; and wherein once the user accepts the estimate or quote from, any of the plurality of 54 

pharmacies, all the pending requests from other plurality of the pharmacies will be canceled automatically or timed out. The method (200) according to Claim 20, wherein the price estimation/quote is an estimation of cost for a corresponding prescription received from the user; and wherein the price quote is dependent on availability of the drugs at the plurality of pharmacies, brand of the drugs and number of medicines required. The method (200) according to Claim 20, wherein the set of pharmacies are optimal to display on the user interface of the user device; and wherein the shortlisting the set of pharmacies depends on at least one of, low-cost estimation, shorter distance, user preferred pharmacies, most preferred pharmacy by other users in the same location and previous user purchase history. The method (200) according to Claim 20, wherein on displaying the cost estimate of the at least one drug in the prescription received from the set the pharmacies on the user interface the user is enabled to accept or reject the price quote from the plurality of pharmacies based on at least one of low-cost estimation, shorter distance, user preferred pharmacies, most preferred pharmacy by other users in the same location and previous user purchase history; and wherein the user interface is equipped with a communication module that enable the user to communicate with the pharmacies to acquire more information on the price quote; and wherein the communication module includes messaging services and/or internet-based call services; and wherein the communication includes negotiation of the price and/or estimated time of delivery; and wherein the user is allowed to choose to pick up the drugs corresponding to the prescription by themselves, or the user may request the pharmacy to deliver to the user location. 55 32. The method (200) according to Claim 20, wherein the price estimation engine comprises additional features including providing price alerts, prescription fill alerts, alerts to notify users on recall notification for a drug or over the counter (OTC) product initiated by authorized government. 33. The method (200) according to Claim 20, wherein the application is configured to support interactions in multiple regional languages including English, Malayalam, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, and Hindi; and wherein the application supports online presence for retail pharmacy shops including displaying of plurality of products of each plurality of pharmacies on the user interface of the user device; and wherein the application allows the user through the user device to shop the products of the plurality of pharmacies remotely. 34. The method (200) according to Claim 20, wherein the price estimation engine provides the user with an option of searching for a desired pharmacy and/or desired price of the at least one drug based on the previous purchase history of the user and/or any other users; and wherein the user can search for a specific pharmacy that sells a specific drug at a lowest price in the given geographic area; and wherein upon finding the pharmacy at lowest price the user is allowed to place the order directly from the identified pharmacy. 35. The method (200) according to Claim 20, wherein the price estimation engine further provides the plurality of pharmacies with an option to carry out a search on the user interface to find at least one drug from other pharmacies in the specified geographic location. 56
